data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service is currently experiencing a high error rate due to repeated Redis connection timeouts, impacting user login sessions.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The qa-login service is encountering session store retrieval failures due to Redis connection timeouts. Each attempt to connect to Redis is timing out after 5000ms, leading to a high error rate of 30.88%.

evidenceChain: High error rate of 30.88% detected in qa-login service.
Loki logs indicate 'LoginException' due to session store retrieval failure.
Logs specify 'Redis connection timeout after 5000ms' as the recurring error message.

timeline.event: First detected 'LoginException' due to session store retrieval failure.
Ongoing 'LoginException' with Redis connection timeout after 5000ms

data.impact: Users are unable to successfully log in due to persistent session retrieval failures, significantly affecting their ability to access the system.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the Redis instance to clear potential orphaned connections, similar to the resolution applied in incident KAN-6.
Investigate the connection pool configuration and timeout settings for Redis to prevent future occurrences.
Implement monitoring for Redis connection health to provide early warnings of similar issues.
